metadata.changes: v4.0.0: Rewrote SKILL.md for agent teams + TypeScript verification scripts (replacing SDK orchestrator). Added entry-mode-aware branching after initial grading found 3 gaps.

expectations.text: Calls detect-framework.ts via Bash (not SDK)
expectations.evidence: Phase 1: exact bash command with npx tsx scripts/detect-framework.ts

expectations.text: Presents work decomposition plan and waits for user confirmation
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 step 4: 'Present the work plan to the user and wait for confirmation'

expectations.text: Calls init-state.ts via Bash with --work-units-json
expectations.evidence: Phase 3: exact bash command with --work-units-json '[...]'

expectations.text: Uses Agent tool (not SDK query) to spawn Test Writer with team_name
expectations.evidence: Step 4a: 'Dispatch a teammate using the Agent tool with team_name: tdd-session'

expectations.text: Calls verify-red.ts via Bash and checks exit code
expectations.evidence: Step 4b: exact bash command, exit 0/1 handling documented

expectations.text: Uses Read tool to read test files from disk for Code Writer (information barrier)
expectations.evidence: Step 4c: 'Use the Read tool to read the test files from disk'

expectations.text: Calls verify-green.ts via Bash with --checksums-json from RED result
expectations.evidence: Step 4d: --checksums-json '{stored_checksums_from_red}'

expectations.text: Spawns all 3 reviewers (spec-compliance, adversarial, code-quality) via Agent tool
expectations.evidence: Steps 4e, 4f, 4g each dispatch via Agent tool with team_name

expectations.text: Calls check-state.ts before generate-report.ts
expectations.evidence: Phase 6: check-state.ts first, then generate-report.ts only if exit 0

expectations.text: No references to Agent SDK, query(), or hooks — pure agent teams + scripts
expectations.evidence: No occurrences of 'Agent SDK', 'query()', or 'hooks' in SKILL.md

expectations.text: Triggers design gate (Phase 0) for multi-feature spec
expectations.evidence: Phase 0: '3+ distinct features' trigger — auth has registration, login, password reset

expectations.text: Decomposes into 3+ work units with dependency ordering
expectations.evidence: Phase 2: work units with dependsOn field

expectations.text: Passes --design-summary to init-state.ts
expectations.evidence: Phase 3: 'Pass --design-summary "{summary}" if Phase 0 ran'

expectations.text: Each unit goes through full RED→GREEN→3-review cycle
expectations.evidence: Steps 4a-4g define the complete pipeline per unit

expectations.text: Respects dependency order (registration before login/reset)
expectations.evidence: Phase 4: 'Units whose dependsOn are all completed can run concurrently'

expectations.text: Uses update-state.ts to track each unit's status transitions
expectations.evidence: Steps 4b, 4d, Completing: update-state.ts called at each transition

expectations.text: Phase 5 runs full test suite (not per-unit)
expectations.evidence: Phase 5: 'Run the full test suite (no specific files)'

expectations.text: Calls check-state.ts and generate-report.ts in Phase 6
expectations.evidence: Phase 6: both script commands present

expectations.text: Uses TeamDelete for cleanup in Phase 7
expectations.evidence: Phase 7: 'Use TeamDelete to shut down the tdd-session team'

expectations.text: Detects entry mode as user-provided-test
expectations.evidence: Phase 1: detectEntryMode returns USER_PROVIDED_TEST for 'implement against *.test.ts'

expectations.text: Skips Test Writer phase — goes straight to RED verification
expectations.evidence: Entry Mode Branching: 'user-provided-test: Skip Step 4a (Test Writer) — go straight to Step 4b (RED verification)'

expectations.text: Calls verify-red.ts with --entry-mode user-provided-test
expectations.evidence: Step 4b: --entry-mode '{mode}' where mode is user-provided-test

expectations.text: Reads existing test file from disk for Code Writer (information barrier)
expectations.evidence: Step 4c: Read tool reads test files from disk

expectations.text: Calls verify-green.ts with checksums from RED result
expectations.evidence: Step 4d: --checksums-json from RED output

expectations.text: Still runs all 3 reviews (does NOT skip reviews for user-provided tests)
expectations.evidence: Entry Mode Branching: 'All other steps (4b-4g) proceed normally'

notes: A2 failed in initial grading, fixed by adding Entry Mode Branching section

expectations.text: Detects entry mode as existing-codebase
expectations.evidence: Phase 1: detectEntryMode detects existing-codebase

expectations.text: Honors --skip-design flag
expectations.evidence: Phase 0: '--skip-design => skip entirely'

expectations.text: Reads existing source files to generate spec-contracts
expectations.evidence: Entry Mode Branching: 'existing-codebase: read the existing source files (Read tool) to understand what code exists before generating spec-contracts'

expectations.text: Calls verify-red.ts with --entry-mode existing-codebase
expectations.evidence: Step 4b: --entry-mode '{mode}'

expectations.text: Handles RED verification for coverage mode (tests may pass initially against existing code)
expectations.evidence: Entry Mode Branching: 'uses a hide-and-restore strategy: temporarily renames impl files so tests fail without them, verifying the tests are genuine. After RED, the impl files are restored'

expectations.text: Calls verify-green.ts after implementation adjustments
expectations.evidence: Step 4d: verify-green.ts command

expectations.text: Report shows coverage additions via generate-report.ts
expectations.evidence: Phase 6: generate-report.ts

notes: A3 and A5 failed in initial grading, fixed by adding Entry Mode Branching section

expectations.text: Triggers design gate (Phase 0) due to ambiguity
expectations.evidence: Phase 0: 'ambiguous requirements' trigger

expectations.text: Asks clarifying questions one at a time (not a questionnaire dump)
expectations.evidence: Phase 0: 'Ask the user one clarifying question at a time (do not dump a list)'

expectations.text: Does NOT call init-state.ts or start Phase 3 before design approval
expectations.evidence: Sequential phase ordering: Phase 3 follows Phase 2 which follows Phase 0

expectations.text: Proposes design approaches with trade-offs
expectations.evidence: Phase 0: 'Propose trade-offs where the spec is ambiguous'

expectations.text: Waits for explicit user confirmation before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 step 4: 'Present the work plan to the user and wait for confirmation'

expectations.text: Design summary feeds into work decomposition (Phase 2)
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 step 1: 'Analyze the spec and design summary (if any)'

expectations.text: Detects entry mode as plan-execution
expectations.evidence: Phase 1: detectEntryMode for 'execute plan'

expectations.text: Classifies tasks into code and non-code types
expectations.evidence: Phase 2: unitType 'code' or 'task'

expectations.text: Routes code tasks through full TDD pipeline (RED→GREEN→reviews)
expectations.evidence: Entry Mode Branching: 'Code units follow the full pipeline (4a-4g)'

expectations.text: Routes non-code tasks through implementer dispatch (spec-compliance + code-quality only)
expectations.evidence: Handling Task Units: implementer + spec compliance + code quality (skip adversarial)

expectations.text: Respects dependency ordering from plan
expectations.evidence: Phase 4: 'Units whose dependsOn are all completed can run concurrently'

expectations.text: Uses eager dispatch (DAG-based, not rigid batches)
expectations.evidence: Phase 4: dependency-based concurrency, not sequential batches

expectations.text: Calls update-state.ts for both code and non-code units
expectations.evidence: Completing a Unit: update-state.ts --status COMPLETED for all unit types

expectations.text: Calls check-state.ts before generate-report.ts
expectations.evidence: Phase 6: sequential check then generate

expectations.text: Phase 5 holistic review covers cross-task integration
expectations.evidence: Phase 5: 'Look for requirements that fell through the cracks between unit boundaries'

expectations.text: Decomposes into 4 work units with no inter-dependencies
expectations.evidence: Phase 2: dependsOn: [] for independent endpoints

expectations.text: Dispatches up to --parallel (default 4) concurrent unit pipelines
expectations.evidence: Arguments: '--parallel <N>: max concurrent unit pipelines (default 4)'

expectations.text: Each unit gets its own Agent tool spawn (Test Writer, Code Writer, reviewers)
expectations.evidence: Steps 4a-4g: per-unit Agent tool dispatches

expectations.text: Each unit calls verify-red.ts and verify-green.ts independently
expectations.evidence: Steps 4b, 4d: per-unit script invocations

expectations.text: All 3 reviews run for each unit (not skipped for parallel batches)
expectations.evidence: Steps 4e-4g: unconditional per-unit reviews

expectations.text: update-state.ts called per-unit as each completes
expectations.evidence: Completing a Unit: update-state.ts per-unit

expectations.text: Phase 5 final integration test covers all 4 endpoints together
expectations.evidence: Phase 5: full test suite with no specific files

delta_from_iteration_2.architecture: v3.0 SDK → v4.0 agent teams + scripts
delta_from_iteration_2.assertions_changed: All 54 assertions are new — v4.0-specific (script invocations, Agent tool, team_name, exit codes)
delta_from_iteration_2.fix_applied: Added Entry Mode Branching section to handle user-provided-test and existing-codebase modes

analyst_observations: v4.0 assertions are fundamentally different from v2.0/v3.0 — they test for agent teams (TeamCreate, Agent tool, team_name) and script invocations (Bash commands with exact script names and args), not SDK calls.

Initial grading found 3 failures, all in the same category: entry-mode-aware branching. The SKILL.md described a generic pipeline but didn't specialize for user-provided-test or existing-codebase modes.
Fix was surgical: a single 'Entry Mode Branching' section (18 lines) before Step 4a that documents all 4 entry modes and their flow differences. All 3 failures now pass.
Strongest structural gates: verify-red.ts and verify-green.ts exit codes. These are Bash commands whose output is in the conversation — the model cannot claim a different result.

Weakest point remains the same as v2.0: review enforcement. Reviews are agent dispatches (model-to-model), not script-verified. The model could theoretically skip a review. However, the 'Red Flags' section and check-state.ts consistency check provide layers of defense.
RECOMMENDATION: Real execution eval needed. Spec-level analysis has been 100% across 3 iterations — the bottleneck is execution compliance, not specification coverage.
